title: Seattle AI Agents & MCP Meetup
description: A hands-on evening exploring the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and how it's changing the way developers build AI agents. We'll cover FastMCP server patterns, tool and resource design, and live demos of MCP servers connecting to real APIs. Attendees will leave with working examples they can build on. Q&A and networking follow the talks.
location: Amazon Meeting Center, 2121 7th Ave, Seattle, WA 98121
talk_title: Building MCP Servers with FastMCP: From Zero to Claude Desktop

title: Kitsap Tech Meetup
description: The monthly gathering of technologists and developers living and working in the Kitsap Peninsula. This month's focus is on AI tooling, local LLM deployment, and integrating AI assistants into everyday workflows. Lightning talks from members, open discussion, and networking at a Bremerton waterfront venue. Ferry commuters welcome — talk starts at 6:30 to give ferry riders time to arrive.
location: Bremerton Harborside Convention Center, 100 Washington Ave, Bremerton, WA 98337

title: Elasticsearch Relevance Summit 2026
description: A two-day conference dedicated to search relevance, vector search, and retrieval-augmented generation. Sessions cover Elastic Inference Service, the Jina model family now native to Elastic, hybrid RRF retrieval pipelines, and production-grade semantic search architectures. Hands-on labs, customer case studies, and an evening reception at Seattle's waterfront. Early registration includes a full workshop day.
location: Hyatt Regency Seattle, 808 Howell St, Seattle, WA 98101
talk_title: Kitsap Commute Helper: Building a Multi-Server MCP App with Elasticsearch and FastMCP

title: Seattle LangChain & RAG Builders Night
description: Monthly meetup for developers building with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and custom RAG pipelines. April's session features a deep dive into agentic retrieval patterns, comparing tool-calling approaches across different LLM providers. Attendees will demo their projects and get feedback from the group. Sponsored by a local AI startup; food and drinks provided.
location: Galvanize Seattle, 111 S Jackson St, Seattle, WA 98104

title: PyCon US 2026 — Pittsburgh
description: The largest annual gathering of the Python community in North America. Five days of talks, tutorials, sprints, and open spaces covering everything from CPython internals to AI/ML frameworks and data engineering. The 2026 theme is 'Python at Scale.' Proposal accepted for a talk on using Python to build MCP servers for AI desktop clients. Full conference pass includes all tutorial days.
location: David L. Lawrence Convention Center, 1000 Fort Duquesne Blvd, Pittsburgh, PA 15222
talk_title: stdio Is the New HTTP: Building AI Tool Servers with FastMCP

title: Eastside Machine Learning Study Group
description: A bi-weekly study group working through current ML papers and implementing key ideas from scratch. May's sessions focus on embedding model architectures — dense retrieval, bi-encoders, and cross-encoders for reranking. Members take turns presenting paper summaries and running code walkthroughs in Jupyter. All levels welcome; discussions stay practical and grounded.
location: Redmond Library, 15990 NE 85th St, Redmond, WA 98052

title: Seattle DevOps & Platform Engineering Summit
description: A full-day event for platform engineers, SREs, and DevOps practitioners. Sessions cover internal developer platforms, AI-assisted incident response, eBPF observability, and GitOps at scale. A morning workshop walks through building a self-service platform with Backstage. Afternoon panels feature engineering leaders from local tech companies. Lunch and breaks included.
location: University of Washington Hub, 4001 E Stevens Way NE, Seattle, WA 98195

title: Bainbridge Island Tech & Ferry Startup Night
description: An intimate evening for founders and technologists who call the Kitsap Peninsula home or commute by ferry. Startup pitches, demo tables, and open conversation about building companies outside of Seattle's core. This month features a presentation on leveraging AI APIs for small teams and a panel on remote-first engineering culture. Hosted at a Bainbridge winery — wine and charcuterie provided.
location: Eleven Winery, 8042 Day Rd E, Bainbridge Island, WA 98110

title: OpenSearch & Elasticsearch Community Day Seattle
description: A community-organized full-day conference for the search and observability community. Talks span OpenSearch contributions, Elasticsearch 9.x features, vector search benchmarks, and the new Elastic Agent Builder platform. The afternoon features hands-on labs with Elastic Serverless and a panel on the Jina AI acquisition's impact on search. Organized by the local Elastic User Group.
location: Tableau HQ, 1621 N 34th St, Seattle, WA 98103
talk_title: Elastic Agent Builder + MCP: Let Claude Search Your Data

title: Seattle JavaScript & TypeScript Meetup
description: Monthly meetup for the JS/TS community in Seattle. June's session covers the TypeScript MCP SDK, building AI-powered browser extensions, and what's new in Node.js 24. We'll have three 20-minute talks followed by open discussion. The TypeScript MCP talk will walk through connecting a custom MCP server to Claude Desktop using the official SDK. Snacks and drinks sponsored by GitHub.

title: AWS Summit Seattle 2026
description: Amazon Web Services' flagship Pacific Northwest event, bringing together cloud practitioners for a day of keynotes, technical sessions, and partner expo. Tracks cover generative AI on AWS, serverless, data & analytics, and security. The AI/ML track features sessions on Bedrock agents, knowledge bases, and integrating third-party tools via the Model Context Protocol. Free to attend with registration.
location: Seattle Convention Center, 705 Pike St, Seattle, WA 98101

title: Kitsap AI & Emerging Tech Showcase
description: An evening showcase of AI projects and emerging technology demos from Kitsap Peninsula developers and researchers. This is a community event open to everyone — attendees will see live demonstrations, vote on their favorite projects, and connect with local technologists. The showcase includes a presentation on building AI commute tools using public transit APIs and language models. Light dinner and drinks provided.
location: Kitsap Conference Center, 100 Washington Ave, Bremerton, WA 98337
talk_title: From Ferry Terminal to AI Agent: Building Kitsap Commute Helper
